questions.text: Who among the following has been awarded as the 'Best actor' (male) at the BRICS film festival 2021?

(a) Habeas-corpus → (i) To transfer a case from lower to higher court
(b) Mandamus → (ii) To look into legality of a post

options.A: the date of declaration of election results
options.B: the date of declaration of election
options.C: the date appointed for its first meeting after elections
options.D: the beginning of first financial year after the Panchayat elections

options.C: the eye is not able to decrease the distance between the eye-lens and the retina beyond a limit.
options.D: the eye is not able to decrease the focal length beyond a limit.

questions.text: Whenever there is a relative motion between a source of sound and an observer, there is apparent change in Frequency of a sound emitted by a source and as heard by the observer. This is called _______.

If the valence shell of an element has only one electron having the following set of quantum numbers: n = 2, l = 0, m = 0, s = +1/2, then the element is _______.

(a) Salamander → (i) Poikilothermic
(b) Duckbilled Platypus → (ii) Sanguivorous

Statement II: Beaveria bassiana is a fungus used as a pesticide.

options.A: Statement I is correct and Statement II is incorrect

questions.text: The search for effective Malarial Vaccine is hampered due to the fact that:

options.A: Plasmodium lives inside cells hidden from immune response

questions.text: Which of the following WBC type has lowest percentage in blood of healthy human?

How does Insulin reduces blood glucose?
(a) Promoting uptake of glucose (b) Glycogen formation
(c) Protein synthesis (d) Fat storage

According to the available data, quantum of pollution caused by various categories of vehicles on the road of certain city reveals that the private four wheeler vehicles cause the maximum pollution, this is followed by the pollution caused by the buses, the trucks and finally the two wheelers.
(a) The number of two wheelers is less than the number of four wheelers plying on the city roads.
(b) Pollution caused by a single private vehicle would be more than the pollution caused by any single bus.

A person walks along a road having lamp post at regular intervals. He took 6 minutes to walk from the first to fourth lamp post. At which lamp post would the person be if he walked continuously at a constant rate for 20 minutes?

[Passage based] There is an increased demand for fish as it is a good source of protein. About 70% of world's marine fish stocks are now overfished. Aquaculture is continuously expanded but it is necessary to manage environmental risks. For this the aquaculture sector needs to devise effective and environment friendly methods.
Select the most appropriate conclusion:

options.A: People should adopt total vegan diet for avoiding environmental problems.
options.B: Aquaculture of predator fishes should be banned till other environment friendly devise is designed for rearing them.
options.C: People should consume freshwater fish varieties to avoid pressure on oceans.
options.D: The farming of only certain kind of varieties is damaging the biodiversity of ecosystems.

questions.text: Ashok jumped higher than Mohan but not higher than Chetan. Naresh jumped higher than Krishna but not as high as that of Mohan. Then what is the correct descending order of players considering their jump?

Some persons are sitting on a bench. V is sitting next to An and S is next to G. G is not sitting with Aj. Aj is on the left end of the bench and S is in second position from right hand end. V is on the right side of An and to the right side of Aj. V and S are sitting together. If G and V exchange their positions select the statement that is not true.

options.A: Positions cannot be ascertained as the direction they face is not known.
options.D: An occupies the second position from one of the end.

questions.text: If 10 people can do a piece of work in 5 days, working 2 hours a day, how long will 2 people take to do the same work, working 5 hours a day?

Given below are 5 statements followed by options composed of 3 statements put together in a specific order. Choose the option that indicates logically valid argument.
(a) Some vehicles are not hazardous to the environment.
(b) Vehicular traffic leads to emission of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases.
(c) Carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases are hazardous to the environment.
(d) Some vehicular traffic does not emit Carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases.
(e) Vehicular traffic is hazardous to the environment.

Replace the question mark (?) by an appropriate alternative in the following series:
32, 33, 37, 46, 62, ___?___
The average age of three person is 60 years. Their ages are in the ratio of 2 : 3 : 4. What is the difference between the ages of the youngest and the eldest person?

A street vendor sold 2 dolls more than half the number of dolls she has on one day. The next day she sold 2 dolls less than half of the remaining dolls available with her. If she was left with 26 dolls.
Select the number of dolls she did initially have.
If the ratio of two numbers is 3 : 4 and the sum of their squares is 400, then which are these numbers?
Neha's salary was first reduced by 10% and then increased by 30% due to upturn. So what percent increase in original salary?

Soma moves 10 km to East and then turns to South and moves 2 km. He again turn East and walks 2 km. After this, he turns to North and walks 7 km. Then the distance from his starting point to the present point is _______.

options.D: Can not be determined from the given data

questions.text: The difference between the simple and compound interest on a certain sum of money for 2 years at 10% per annum is ₹ 750. What is the sum?
